title: Clusia nigrolineata
taxonomy: Clusia nigrolineata belongs to the family Clusiaceae and the order Malpighiales. It is a species of flowering plant in the genus Clusia. The classification of Clusia nigrolineata is within the kingdom Plantae. The plant's classification is further divided into the class Magnoliopsida and the order Malpighiales. This classification helps to understand the plant's evolutionary relationships and characteristics.
uses: Clusia nigrolineata has been utilized in traditional medicine in various regions, but its specific medicinal properties and uses are not well-documented. Further research is needed to fully understand the potential medicinal uses of Clusia nigrolineata.

intro: The Clusia nigrolineata, also known as the Black-lined Clusia, is a plant species within the family Clusiaceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The health benefits of Clusia nigrolineata include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments, including wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
